commit_scope: fourth-kernel.form m4d first move — the quine seed: four axiom-faced ops grow the emitted walker (NODE observe-itself, PUTC efferent port, DIV/MOD figure leaves), and a self-printer program, authored by closing the quine knot (it contains its own true length), walks its OWN node table in the booted binary and emits it byte-exactly. Band three-way at 15; the byte-exact fixpoint check rides the audit.

commands: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/minimal-surface.fk form-stdlib/fourth-walker.fk form-stdlib/fourth-walker-emit.fk form-stdlib/tests/fourth-fixpoint-band.fk
cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/minimal-surface.fk form-stdlib/fourth-walker.fk form-stdlib/fourth-walker-emit.fk form-stdlib/tests/fourth-walker-emit-band.fk
local_validation.summary: fourth-fixpoint-band holds three-way at 15: (1) the new ops flatten canonically (PUTC and DIV tables); (2) knot stability — the printer's row count with the placeholder equals the count with the true length, so the fixpoint lands in one rebuild; (4) deterministic self-printer emission; (8) the knot's literal is present — the program's table carries LIT(N-1), its own true bound. The walker-emit band re-proven at 15 with the grown walker text (arms to 11). The audit's m4d section booted the self-printer: the binary emitted its own 283-row node table byte-exactly — 3388 bytes — with the probe narrating from inside: PUTC 3388 (== byte count), NODE 3176 self-reads, SELF 284 (rows+1), DIV/MOD 547 each.

observed_delta.probe_arms: LIT 9720, ARG 3745, ADD 5354, SUB 0, LE 1778, IF 1778, SELF 284, NODE 3176, PUTC 3388, DIV 547, MOD 547
observed_delta.probe_invariant: PUTC count == emitted byte count; SELF == rows + 1

known_limitations.limitation: The quine seed emits the data half (the node table); the constant walker text is not yet self-emitted — that needs a string-pool streaming lane in the emitted C.
known_limitations.follow_up: Stream the walker text through PUTC from a pool the emitter lays down; then the binary emits its whole source.

known_limitations.limitation: Ops 8-11 live in the emitted walker; the recipe-level fk-walk stays ops 1-7 (no putchar-shaped native at recipe level) — parity for self-print programs is the audit's text-diff against the emitter's truth, not a recipe-walk.
known_limitations.follow_up: If a recipe-level efferent-char port lands, fk-walk grows the same arms and the band gains recipe-walk parity claims.

known_limitations.limitation: The full m4d fixpoint — the emitter itself authored as a program the walker runs on itself — remains open; SELF gives one recursion, the emitter wants several (mode-packing or a CALL op is the path).
known_limitations.follow_up: Grow toward CALL/multi-function or mode-packed encoding; the fixpoint band: same content-address in, byte-identical source out.

ci_validation.summary: PR gates run both bands three-way.

deploy_validation.summary: form-stdlib additions ride the api image rebuild; the lattice ingests the updated fourth-kernel.form on the post-merge hook.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: No route behavior changes. The emitted sibling gains self-observation (NODE) and self-emission (PUTC): the booted binary reproduces its own program table byte-exactly with the trace narrating — the data half of m4d's self-hosting fixpoint, measured.
test_flows: fourth-fixpoint-band proves new-op flatten canonicals, knot stability, deterministic emission, and the knot literal present, three-way at 15.
fourth_kernel_audit.sh m4d section: emit self-printer -> clang -> run -> diff the binary's self-emitted table against fkc-rows-text of the same flatten -> byte-exact, probe shown.
e2e_validation.summary: validate.sh both bands 1 ok 0 divergent; audit end-to-end fixpoint diff byte-exact.

phase_gate.gate: the data half self-emits byte-exactly with the probe narrating; CI three-way + deploy ingest confirm the crossing
phase_gate.next: stream the constant walker text (string-pool lane) -> the binary emits its whole source; then the emitter as a program — self-hosting whole
